name: Laguna de San Pedro Mallo – circular from Matarrosa del Sil via Cerro de la Laguna
name_original: Laguna de San Pedro Mallo – circular desde Matarrosa del Sil por el Cerro de la Laguna
region_intro: Hiking around Toreno offers diverse landscapes, characterized by mountainous terrain, hills, and woodlands. The region features notable peaks and elevated areas, providing varied hiking experiences. Trails often lead through natural environments, including areas with lagoons and viewpoints. This varied topography makes Toreno a suitable destination for different types of outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Toreno The most popular hiking route is Zofreral de Corbana – circular, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores a forested area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna de San Pedro Mallo – circular from Matarrosa del Sil via Cerro de la Laguna, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.8 km) path. This trail features a lagoon and a hill, offering scenic views. Local hikers also love the La Gualta Viewpoint – Gualta Waterfall loop from Noceda, a 5.9 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a viewpoint and waterfall, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Hiking around Toreno is defined by mountainous areas, hills, and natural water features like lagoons. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Toreno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Toreno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Toreno?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes around Toreno, offering a variety of experiences. These include paths through mountainous terrain, hills, and woodlands, with options ranging from moderate to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Toreno?
A: Hikes around Toreno feature diverse terrain, including mountainous areas, rolling hills, and lush woodlands. You'll find paths leading through natural environments, sometimes with lagoons and scenic viewpoints. Some routes involve significant elevation changes, particularly those ascending peaks like Pico Catoute.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Toreno?
A: While many routes in Toreno are moderate to difficult, the region does offer some paths suitable for families. These trails often lead through varied terrain and are generally well-indicated. For specific easy routes, it's best to check individual trail descriptions on komoot.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Toreno?
A: Many natural areas and trails in Toreno are generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Specific regulations might apply in certain protected zones, so checking local signage is advisable.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Toreno area?
A: Yes, Toreno offers several circular hiking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051248904/zofreral-de-corbana-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zofreral de Corbana – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which explores a forested area. Another well-liked loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027265703/laguna-de-san-pedro-mallo-circular-from-matarrosa-del-sil-via-cerro-de-la-laguna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de San Pedro Mallo – circular from Matarrosa del Sil via Cerro de la Laguna&lt;/a&gt;, featuring a lagoon and scenic views.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can discover on a hike?
A: Hikers can discover a variety of natural features and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1276601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Gualta Viewpoint – Gualta Waterfall loop from Noceda&lt;/a&gt; offers both a scenic vista and a waterfall. You might also encounter lagoons, such as those near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Rebeza&lt;/a&gt;, and enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6183733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Dam on the Sil River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible via hiking trails?
A: While specific historical trails like the Via Crucis or Via Francigena are found in other parts of the broader region, Toreno itself offers access to interesting landmarks. You can find religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4838103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Esteban de Columbrianos&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4838109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Blas and San Roque&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5550678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Vega de Espinareda&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Toreno?
A: The hiking routes in Toreno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 25 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain and the natural beauty of the area, making it a popular choice for outdoor exploration.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads around Toreno?
A: Many trailheads in and around Toreno, especially those for popular routes, typically offer designated parking areas. However, it's always a good idea to check the specific route details on komoot for information on parking availability at your chosen starting point.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Toreno?
A: The best time for hiking in Toreno generally depends on the specific trail and desired conditions. Spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter might present opportunities for snowshoeing or winter hiking in higher mountainous areas, requiring appropriate gear and experience.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, Toreno offers several challenging hikes for experienced trekkers. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14985857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Catoute (2,117 m) – Pico de la Cernella (2,119 m) loop from Salentinos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14985907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Catoute (2,117 m) loop from Salentinos&lt;/a&gt; involve significant elevation gains and longer distances, often taking over 6 hours to complete. These routes are classified as difficult and require good fitness.

Q: Can I find cafes or pubs near the hiking trails in Toreno?
A: While the immediate trailheads might be in natural settings, many villages and towns in the Toreno region, including Toreno itself, offer cafes, pubs, and accommodation options where hikers can rest and refuel. Planning your route to pass through or end near such a village is recommended.
tips: Near the town of Villamartín del Sil, we find this beautiful dirt road. Without a doubt, a beautiful picture surrounded by nature, quite possibly walking towards the San Pedro Mallo Lagoon.

Located in an environment of pure nature and tranquility, the small lagoon of San Pedro Mallo is an ideal place to relax and follow a very pleasant and relaxed itinerary. Very close to here is the recreational area with everything you need to have a picnic if you prefer.
